message:
docs: fix inaccuracies found in README and docs audit

- README: fix missing WASM files in tree (arrows, compress, font)
- README: fix elbowed default claim and message() alias description
- architecture.mdx: update to 3 MCP tools, complete WASM exports table
- architecture.mdx: fix splines description (ortho for TB/BT, curved for LR/RL)
- architecture.mdx: add sdk-types.ts and missing zig files to tree
- getting-started.mdx: update to mention all 3 tools
- sdk-reference.mdx: add missing toCode() section
- sdk-reference.mdx: fix elbowed default documentation

files.patch:
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ const d = new Diagram(opts?: {
 | Method | Description |
 |--------|-------------|
 | `connect(from, to, label?, opts?)` | Connect two elements with an arrow. |
-| `message(from, to, label?, opts?)` | Sequence diagram message (alias for connect). |
+| `message(from, to, label?, opts?)` | Sequence diagram message. Uses separate message queue for sequence layout. |
 
 ### Querying
 
@@ -181,7 +181,7 @@ interface ConnectOpts {

   startArrowhead?: null | "arrow" | "bar" | "dot" | "triangle" | "diamond" | "diamond_outline";
   endArrowhead?: null | "arrow" | "bar" | "dot" | "triangle" | "diamond" | "diamond_outline";   // default "arrow"
-  elbowed?: boolean;                 // default true (orthogonal routing)
+  elbowed?: boolean;                 // orthogonal routing (Graphviz handles routing by default)
   labelFontSize?: number;
   labelPosition?: "start" | "middle" | "end";
   customData?: Record<string, unknown>;

@@ -5,9 +5,9 @@ description: How drawmode works — execution pipeline, layout engine, and WASM
 
 ## Code Mode Pattern
 
-drawmode exposes a single `draw` MCP tool. The LLM writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
+drawmode exposes three MCP tools: `draw` (generate/edit diagrams), `draw_describe` (convert `.excalidraw` to TypeScript), and `draw_info` (capabilities reference). The LLM writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
 
-1. LLM receives one tool (`draw`) with SDK type definitions (~100 lines)
+1. LLM receives the `draw` tool with SDK type definitions (~100 lines)
 2. LLM writes code using the `Diagram` class
 3. `executor.ts` runs code via `new Function("Diagram", code)` with a `ConfiguredDiagram` subclass
 4. Zig WASM (Graphviz C statically linked) handles layout positioning and arrow routing via the `dot` engine

 │   ├── upload.ts            # Excalidraw.com upload (encrypt + POST)
 │   ├── png.ts               # Image export (PNG/SVG via puppeteer + Excalidraw CDN)
 │   ├── types.ts             # Shared types (ColorPreset, ShapeOpts, ConnectOpts, etc.)
+│   ├── sdk-types.ts         # SDK type definitions string (embedded in tool description)
 │   └── widget.html          # MCP Apps HTML widget for Claude Desktop/Cowork
 ├── wasm/                    # Zig WASM module
 │   ├── src/

 │   │   ├── layout.zig       # Graphviz layout (C lib statically linked)
-│   │   ├── arrows.zig       # Arrow routing (unused — Graphviz handles routing)
+│   │   ├── arrows.zig       # Arrow routing
 │   │   ├── validate.zig     # Structural validation
+│   │   ├── compress.zig     # Zlib compression (RFC 1950/1951)
+│   │   ├── font.zig         # Font metrics
 │   │   └── util.zig         # Shared utilities
 │   └── build.zig
 ├── worker/                  # Cloudflare Worker entry point (remote MCP)
@@ -47,7 +50,7 @@ drawmode/
 The primary layout engine is Graphviz — the C library is statically linked into the Zig WASM module (`drawmode.wasm`).
 
 - **`dot` layout**: Layered graph layout (Sugiyama algorithm) with proper crossing minimization
-- **`splines=ortho`**: Orthogonal edge routing (90-degree elbows matching Excalidraw style), falls back to `splines=polyline` if ortho fails
+- **`splines=ortho`**: Orthogonal edge routing for vertical layouts (TB/BT); curved splines for horizontal layouts (LR/RL)
 - **Cluster subgraphs**: Groups rendered as Graphviz clusters for proper containment
 - **`rank=same`**: Nodes with same `row` value share a rank in the layout
 - **Coordinate conversion**: Graphviz points (Y-up) → Excalidraw pixels (Y-down)
@@ -60,8 +63,12 @@ A single Zig WASM binary with Graphviz C statically linked. Handles layout, edge
 
 | Export | Description |
 |--------|-------------|
+| `alloc` / `dealloc` | Bump allocator memory management |
+| `resetHeap` | Reset bump allocator between calls |
 | `layoutGraph` | Primary layout engine (Graphviz Sugiyama with orthogonal edge routing) |
+| `routeArrows` | Arrow endpoint routing |
 | `validate` | Structural validation (bound text, duplicate IDs, overlapping elements) |
+| `zlibCompress` | Zlib compression for excalidraw.com upload |
 
 Input/output is JSON bytes — the TypeScript layer serializes to JSON, passes to WASM, and reads back results.

@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@ Add this to your MCP client's configuration file:
 
 ## Your First Diagram
 
-Once configured, ask your LLM to draw a diagram. The LLM receives a single `draw` tool and writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
+Once configured, ask your LLM to draw a diagram. The LLM receives three tools — `draw` (generate/edit), `draw_describe` (convert existing `.excalidraw` to code), and `draw_info` (capabilities reference) — and writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
 
 ```typescript
 const d = new Diagram();

@@ -273,6 +273,31 @@ Add a sequence diagram actor. Returns the actor ID. Uses the same `ShapeOpts` as
 
 Add a message between actors. Uses the same `ConnectOpts` as `connect`. Self-messages (same actor) render as rectangular loops.
 
+## Decompiler
+
+### `toCode(opts?)`
+
+Convert the current diagram state back to TypeScript SDK code. Produces compact, readable code that recreates the diagram — useful for debugging, sharing, and editing existing diagrams.
+
+```typescript
+const code = d.toCode();
+// Returns TypeScript like:
+// const d = new Diagram();
+// const api = d.addBox("API Gateway", { row: 0, col: 1, color: "backend" });
+// ...
+
+// With a file path for render output:
+const code = d.toCode({ path: "architecture.excalidraw" });
+```
+
+Features:
+- Reverse-maps hex colors back to named presets (`"#d0bfff"` → `"backend"`)
+- Generates camelCase variable names from labels
+- Extracts shared styles when 3+ nodes share the same non-positional options
+- Avoids JavaScript reserved words in variable names
+
+A `.drawmode.ts` sidecar file containing `toCode()` output is automatically written alongside any file output from `render()`.
+
 ## Mermaid Import
 
 ### `Diagram.fromMermaid(syntax)`
@@ -383,7 +408,7 @@ Options for `connect()`:
 | `opacity` | `number` | — | 0-100 |
 | `startArrowhead` | `Arrowhead` | `null` | Start arrowhead type |
 | `endArrowhead` | `Arrowhead` | `"arrow"` | End arrowhead type |
-| `elbowed` | `boolean` | `true` | Use elbow routing |
+| `elbowed` | `boolean` | — | Orthogonal routing (Graphviz handles routing by default) |
 | `labelFontSize` | `number` | — | Label font size |
 | `labelPosition` | `"start" \| "middle" \| "end"` | `"middle"` | Where to place the edge label along the arrow |
 | `customData` | `Record<string, unknown> \| null` | — | Arbitrary metadata |
